text:
{
  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a venerated icon of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.",
      "It is an icon of Orthodox Christianity.",
      "It is a Byzantine icon."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has the Virgin Mary shown as the \"Hodegetria\" version.",
      "It has Mary directing attention away from herself.",
      "It has Mary gesturing with her right hand toward Jesus.",
      "It has the child extending his right hand toward the viewer in blessing.",
      "It has the child holding a book of gospels in his left hand.",
      "It has Mary in fleur-de-lis robes.",
      "It has two scars on the right cheek."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by a traditional composition well known in the icons of Orthodox Christianity.",
      "It is characterized by fleur-de-lis robes.",
      "It is characterized by two deep strikes on the right cheek."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for veneration by both Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox Christians.",
      "It is used for pilgrimage.",
      "It is used for devotion."
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "It is located at the Jasna Góra Monastery in Częstochowa, Poland."
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "unknown"

      "It is caused by a robber inflicting two deep strikes with a sword."
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "It affects the course of the war according to some accounts.",
      "It affects the aesthetic of the icon when gemstones are repositioned."
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "It consists of the Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.",
      "It consists of a wooden panel backing.",
      "It consists of tempera infused with diluted wax."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of the Jasna Góra Monastery.",
      "It is part of the Catholic Church in Poland.",
      "It is part of the Eastern Orthodox Churches.",
      "It is part of the \"From Ocean to Ocean\" pilgrimage."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [

    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "It belongs to the Jasna Góra Monastery.",
      "It belongs to the Roman Catholic Church.",
      "It belongs to the Eastern Orthodox Christians."
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for being a venerated icon of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.",
      "It is known for its veneration by both Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox Christians.",
      "It is known for its miraculous saving of the monastery of Jasna Góra from a Swedish invasion.",
      "It is known for the two scars on its right cheek.",
      "It is known for being the most popular shrine in Poland.",
      "It is known for its canonical coronation by Pope Clement XI.",
      "It is known for receiving three Pontifical golden roses."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with Poland for the past 600 years.",
      "It is associated with Luke the Evangelist.",
      "It is associated with Helena.",
      "It is associated with Constantine the Great.",
      "It is associated with Władysław Opolczyk, Duke of Opole.",
      "It is associated with Louis of Anjou, King of Poland and Hungary.",
      "It is associated with King Lev I of Galicia.",
      "It is associated with the Anjou dynasty.",
      "It is associated with the Paulites, a hermitic order from Hungary.",
      "It is associated with the \"From Ocean to Ocean\" pilgrimage."
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "It occurred in 1430 when damaged by robbers.",
      "It occurred in 1655 during the Siege of Jasna Góra.",
      "It occurred in 1 April 1656 when proclaimed Queen of Poland.",
      "It occurred in 1909 when the crowns were stolen.",
      "It occurred in 1910 when the crowns were replaced.",
      "It occurred in 1978 when Pope John Paul II gifted golden roses.",
      "It occurred in 2005 when Pope John Paul II gifted crowns.",
      "It occurred in 2006 when Pope Benedict XVI gifted golden roses.",
      "It occurred in 2016 when Pope Francis gifted golden roses.",
      "It occurred in 1384 when Władysław was passing Częstochowa."

      "It was held in the Pauline monastery in Mochów.",
      "It was held in the cathedral of Lwów.",
      "It was held in the Vatican."

      "unknown"

      "It is about the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus."
    ],

      "It was influenced by Orthodox Christianity.",
      "It was influenced by the \"Hodegetria\" version of icons.",
      "It was influenced by the French royal coat of arms.",
      "It was influenced by the Anjou dynasty."
    ],
    "Influenced": [

      "It influenced the proclamation of the Virgin as Queen of Poland."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それは聖母マリアと幼子イエスを崇敬するイコンです。",
      "それは正教会のイコンです。",
      "それはビザンチン様式のイコンです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは「ホデゲトリア」様式の聖母マリアが描かれています。",
      "それは聖母マリアが自分自身から注意をそらしています。",
      "それは聖母マリアが右手をイエスに向けて指し示しています。",
      "それは幼子が鑑賞者に向けて右手を祝福の形で伸ばしています。",
      "それは幼子が左手に福音書を holding しています。",
      "それは聖母マリアがフルール・ド・リスの衣装を着ています。",
      "それは右頬に二つの傷があります。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは正教会のイコンでよく知られた伝統的な構図を特徴としています。",
      "それはフルール・ド・リスの衣装を特徴としています。",
      "それは右頬の二つの深い切り傷を特徴としています。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはローマ・カトリック教徒と東方正教徒双方による崇敬に使われます。",
      "それは巡礼に使われます。",
      "それは崇敬に使われます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはポーランドのチェンストホヴァにあるヤスナ・グラ修道院にあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それはエルサレムで生まれました。",
      "それはコンスタンティノープルで生まれました。",
      "それは聖家族の家で生まれました。",
      "それは6世紀または9世紀に生まれました。"
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "それは伝説によれば福音記者ルカによって作成されました。",
      "それは中世の修復家によって作成されました。"
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "それは美術史家によれば6世紀または9世紀に作成されました。",
      "それは約6世紀または9世紀に作成されました。"
    ],
    "DevelopedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DevelopedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DiscoveredBy": [
      "それはヘレナによってエルサレムで発見されました。"
    ],
    "DiscoveredIn": [
      "それは326年にエルサレムで発見されました。"
    ],
    "InventedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "InventedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PublishedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "WrittenBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ComposedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DirectedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PerformedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ManufacturedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ProducedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "それは1430年に強盗によって元の絵が損傷したことによって引き起こされました。",
      "それは1430年にフス派が聖域を略奪したことによって引き起こされました。",
      "それは強盗が剣で右頬に二つの深い切り傷を負わせたことによって引き起こされました。"
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "それは一部の記述によれば戦争の経過に影響を与えます。",
      "それは宝石が配置し直される際にイコンの美観に影響を与えます。"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それは聖母マリアと幼子イエスで構成されています。",
      "それは木製のパネルの裏打ちで構成されています。",
      "それはワックスを混ぜたテンペラで構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それはヤスナ・グラ修道院の一部です。",
      "それはポーランドのカトリック教会の一部です。",
      "それは東方正教会の一部です。",
      "それは「海から海へ」の巡礼の一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "それはヤスナ・グラ修道院に属しています。",
      "それはローマ・カトリック教会に属しています。",
      "それは東方正教徒に属しています。"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それは聖母マリアと幼子イエスの崇敬されるイコンとして知られています。",
      "それはローマ・カトリック教徒と東方正教徒双方による崇敬で知られています。",
      "それはヤスナ・グラ修道院をスウェーデン侵攻から奇跡的に救ったことで知られています。",
      "それは右頬の二つの傷で知られています。",
      "それはポーランドで最も人気のある聖地として知られています。",
      "それはクレメンス11世教皇による教会法上の戴冠式で知られています。",
      "それは3つの教皇による黄金のバラを受けたことで知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それは過去600年間ポーランドと関連付けられています。",
      "それは福音記者ルカと関連付けられています。",
      "それはヘレナと関連付けられています。",
      "それはコンスタンティヌス大帝と関連付けられています。",
      "それはオポーレ公ヴワディスワフと関連付けられています。",
      "それはポーランドとハンガリーの王ルイ1世と関連付けられています。",
      "それはガリツィア王レフ1世と関連付けられています。",
      "それはアンジュー家と関連付けられています。",
      "それはハンガリーの隠遁修道会であるパウロ会と関連付けられています。",
      "それは「海から海へ」の巡礼と関連付けられています。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それは1430年に強盗によって損傷した際に起こりました。",
      "それは1655年のヤスナ・グラ包囲戦中に起こりました。",
      "それは1656年4月1日にポーランドの女王として宣言された際に起こりました。",
      "それは1909年に王冠が盗まれた際に起こりました。",
      "それは1910年に王冠が交換された際に起こりました。",
      "それは1978年にヨハネ・パウロ2世教皇が黄金のバラを贈った際に起こりました。",
      "それは2005年にヨハネ・パウロ2世教皇が王冠を贈った際に起こりました。",
      "それは2006年にベネディクト16世教皇が黄金のバラを贈った際に起こりました。",
      "それは2016年にフランシスコ教皇が黄金のバラを贈った際に起こりました。",
      "それは1384年にヴワディスワフがチェンストホヴァを通過した際に起こりました。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それはヤスナ・グラ修道院で保持されていました。",
      "それはルブリニェツの城で保持されていました。",
      "それはモフのパウロ会修道院で保持されていました。",
      "それはルヴフの大聖堂で保持されていました。",
      "それはバチカンで保持されていました。"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それは聖母マリアと幼子イエスについてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それは正教会によって影響を受けました。",
      "それはイコンの「ホデゲトリア」様式によって影響を受けました。",
      "それはフランス王家の紋章によって影響を受けました。",
      "それはアンジュー家によって影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "それは戦争の経過に影響を与えました。",
      "それはヨハネ・カジミール・ヴァザ王にルヴフの誓いを立てるよう影響を与えました。",
      "それは聖母のポーランド女王としての宣言に影響を与えました。"
    ]
  }
}

IsA: It is a venerated icon of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.
It is an icon of Orthodox Christianity.
HasA: It has the Virgin Mary shown as the "Hodegetria" version.
It has Mary directing attention away from herself.
It has Mary gesturing with her right hand toward Jesus.
It has the child extending his right hand toward the viewer in blessing.
It has the child holding a book of gospels in his left hand.
It has Mary in fleur-de-lis robes.
It has two scars on the right cheek.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a traditional composition well known in the icons of Orthodox Christianity.
It is characterized by fleur-de-lis robes.
It is characterized by two deep strikes on the right cheek.
UsedFor: It is used for veneration by both Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox Christians.
LocatedIn: It is located at the Jasna Góra Monastery in Częstochowa, Poland.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the house of the Holy Family.
It originated in the sixth or ninth century.
CreatedBy: It was created by Luke the Evangelist according to legend.
It was created by medieval restorers.
CreatedIn: It was created in the sixth or ninth century according to art historians.
It was created around the sixth or ninth century.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by Helena in Jerusalem.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered in Jerusalem in 326.
CausedBy: It is caused by robbers damaging the original image in 1430.
It is caused by Hussites plundering the sanctuary in 1430.
It is caused by a robber inflicting two deep strikes with a sword.
Affects: It affects the course of the war according to some accounts.
It affects the aesthetic of the icon when gemstones are repositioned.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.
It consists of a wooden panel backing.
It consists of tempera infused with diluted wax.
PartOf: It is part of the Jasna Góra Monastery.
It is part of the Catholic Church in Poland.
It is part of the Eastern Orthodox Churches.
It is part of the "From Ocean to Ocean" pilgrimage.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Jasna Góra Monastery.
It belongs to the Roman Catholic Church.
It belongs to the Eastern Orthodox Christians.
KnownFor: It is known for being a venerated icon of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.
It is known for its veneration by both Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox Christians.
It is known for its miraculous saving of the monastery of Jasna Góra from a Swedish invasion.
It is known for the two scars on its right cheek.
It is known for being the most popular shrine in Poland.
It is known for its canonical coronation by Pope Clement XI.
It is known for receiving three Pontifical golden roses.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Poland for the past 600 years.
It is associated with Luke the Evangelist.
It is associated with Helena.
It is associated with Constantine the Great.
It is associated with Władysław Opolczyk, Duke of Opole.
It is associated with Louis of Anjou, King of Poland and Hungary.
It is associated with King Lev I of Galicia.
It is associated with the Anjou dynasty.
It is associated with the Paulites, a hermitic order from Hungary.
It is associated with the "From Ocean to Ocean" pilgrimage.
OccurredIn: It occurred in 1430 when damaged by robbers.
It occurred in 1655 during the Siege of Jasna Góra.
It occurred in 1 April 1656 when proclaimed Queen of Poland.
It occurred in 1909 when the crowns were stolen.
It occurred in 1910 when the crowns were replaced.
It occurred in 1978 when Pope John Paul II gifted golden roses.
It occurred in 2005 when Pope John Paul II gifted crowns.
It occurred in 2006 when Pope Benedict XVI gifted golden roses.
It occurred in 2016 when Pope Francis gifted golden roses.
It occurred in 1384 when Władysław was passing Częstochowa.
HeldIn: It was held in the Jasna Góra Monastery.
It was held in the castle in Lubliniec.
It was held in the Pauline monastery in Mochów.
It was held in the cathedral of Lwów.
It was held in the Vatican.
About: It is about the Blessed Virgin Mary and the Child Jesus.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Orthodox Christianity.
It was influenced by the "Hodegetria" version of icons.
It was influenced by the French royal coat of arms.
It was influenced by the Anjou dynasty.
Influenced: It influenced the course of the war.
It influenced King John II Casimir Vasa to give the Lwów Oath.
It influenced the proclamation of the Virgin as Queen of Poland.
